title: Brute-force authentication attack detected
description: 462 failed login attempts against user j.adams@acmecorp.com from IP 198.51.100.47 within 8 minutes, followed by a successful login. Source IP geo-locates to a known bulletproof hosting provider. MFA was not enforced on this account.

title: Lateral movement via PsExec detected
description: Process psexec.exe spawned on WORKSTATION-117 by user svc_deploy, initiating remote service creation on 4 internal hosts (10.0.2.15, 10.0.2.16, 10.0.2.20, 10.0.3.5). The service account svc_deploy is not authorised for interactive use. Parent process is cmd.exe launched from an abnormal directory (C:\Users\Public\Downloads).

title: Privilege escalation via token manipulation
description: Process whoami.exe executed with SYSTEM privileges after token impersonation detected on SRV-APP-03. A low-privilege service account (svc_webapp) used SeDebugPrivilege to duplicate an SYSTEM-level token from lsass.exe. This technique is consistent with tools such as Potato or PrintSpoofer.

title: Anomalous data exfiltration to external endpoint
description: Host 10.0.5.22 (FILE-SVR-02) transferred 4.7 GB to external IP 203.0.113.88 over HTTPS in a 12-minute window during non-business hours (05:30-05:42 UTC). DNS lookups resolved to a domain registered 48 hours ago (xfer-cdn-node9[.]com). The volume exceeds the host's 90-day baseline by 47x.

title: Malware execution – Cobalt Strike beacon detected
description: A Cobalt Strike beacon payload was identified in memory on WORKSTATION-204. The process rundll32.exe loaded a reflective DLL (sha256: a1b2c3d4e5f6…) matching known Cobalt Strike malleable C2 profile signatures. The beacon is calling back to 192.0.2.33:443 every 60 seconds with jitter. The initial dropper was delivered via a macro-enabled Excel document (Q1-Financial-Report.xlsm) opened by user m.chen@acmecorp.com.
